spells.desc: You summon a fey creature of CR 6 or lower, or a fey spirit that takes the form of a beast of CR 6 or lower. It appears in an unoccupied space that you can see within range. If you lose concentration, the creature doesn't disappear and you lose control of it.

spells.name: Contingency

material.desc: a statuette of yourself carved from ivory and decorated with gems worth 1,500 GP

spells.desc: Choose a spell of 5th level or lower that you can cast, that has a casting time of 1 action, and that can target you. Cast that contingent spell (without spending a spell slot), and name a trigger. The contingent spell is cast when the trigger occurs.

spells.name: Create Undead

material.desc: one clay pot filled with grave dirt, one clay pot filled with brackish water, and one 150 GP black onyx stone for each corpse

spells.desc: You can cast this spell only at night. Choose up to three corpses of Medium or Small humanoids within range. Each corpse becomes a ghoul under your control. On each of your turns, you can mentally command any creature you animaged with this spell.

spells.name: Disintegrate

material.desc: a lodestone and a pinch of dust

spells.desc: A thin green ray springs from your pointing finger to a target you can see within range. The target can be a creature, an object, or a creation of magical force. A creature targeted must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 10d6+40 force damage on a fail. If this damage reduces it to 0 HP, it is disintegrated.

spells.name: Eyebite
spells.desc: For the duration, your eyes become an inky void imbued with dread power. One creature of your choice within 60 feet of you that you can see must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be affected with one of the following until the spell ends: Asleep, Panicked, or Sickened.

spells.name: Find the Path

material.desc: a set of divination tools worth 100 GP and an object from the location you seek

spells.desc: This spell allows you to find the shortest, most direct physical route to a specific fixed location that you are familiar with on the same plane of existence. If you name a destination on another plane, in a moving location, or a destination that doesn't exist, the spell fails.

spells.name: Flesh to Stone

material.desc: a pinch of lime, water, and earth

spells.desc: You attempt to turn one creature that you can see within range into stone. If the target's body is made of flesh, the creature must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it is restrained. While restrained this way, the target must repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns. On three failures, it is turned to stone permanently.

spells.name: Forbiddance

material.desc: a sprinkling of holy water, rare incense, and powdered ruby worth 1,000 GP

spells.desc: You create a ward against magical travel that protects up to 40,000 square feet of floor space to a height of 30 feet above the floor. For the duration, creatures can't teleport into the area or use portals to enter the area. The ward also prevents planar travel. Damage type determined by alignment.

spells.name: Globe of Invulnerability

material.desc: a glass or crystal bead that shatters when the spell ends

spells.desc: An immobile, faintly shimmering barrier springs into existence in a 10-foot radius around you and remains for the duration. Any spell of 5th level or lower cast from outside the barrier can't affect creatures or objects within it, even if the spell is cast using a higher level slot.

spells.name: Guards and Wards

material.desc: burning incense, a small measure of brimstone and oil, a knotted string, a small amount of umber hulk blood, and a small silver rod worth 10 GP

spells.desc: You create a ward that protects up to 2,500 square feet of floor space. Multiple effects protect this area: magical fog, locked doors, magical darkness, false doors, and other obstacles. The whole warded area radiates magic.

spells.name: Harm
spells.desc: You unleash a virulent disease on a creature within range. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 14d6 necrotic damage. The target's hit point maximum is reduced by the amount of necrotic damage taken until a long rest. This reduction can't reduce the target's maximum below 1.

spells.name: Heal
spells.desc: Choose a creature you can see within range. A surge of positive energy washes through the creature, causing it to regain 70 hit points. This spell also ends blindness, deafness, and any diseases affecting the target. This spell has no effect on constructs or undead.

spells.name: Heroes' Feast

material.desc: a gem-encrusted bowl worth 1,000 GP, which the spell consumes

spells.desc: You bring forth a great feast, including magnificent food and drink. The feast takes 1 hour to consume and disappears at the end of that time, and the beneficial effects don't set in until this hour is over. Up to twelve creatures can partake of the feast. They gain immunity to poison and disease, Wisdom saving throw advantage, and 2d10 temporary HP.

spells.name: Magic Jar

material.desc: a gem, crystal, reliquary, or some other ornamental container worth 500 GP

spells.desc: Your body falls into a catatonic state as your soul leaves it and enters the container. While your soul inhabits the container, you can telepathically sense any humanoid within 100 feet. You can attempt to possess a humanoid's body by forcing a Charisma saving throw.

spells.name: Mass Suggestion

material.desc: a snake's tongue and either a bit of honeycomb or a drop of sweet oil

spells.desc: You suggest a course of activity (limited to a sentence or two) and magically influence up to twelve creatures of your choice that you can see within range, which can hear and understand you. Creatures that can't be charmed are immune. The suggestion must be worded so as to make the course of action sound reasonable.

spells.name: Move Earth

material.desc: an iron blade and a small bag containing a mixture of soils—clay, loam, and sand

spells.desc: Choose an area of terrain no larger than 40 feet on a side within range. You can reshape dirt, sand, or clay in the area in any manner you choose for the duration. You can raise or lower the area's elevation, create or fill in a trench, erect or flatten a wall, or form a pillar.

spells.name: Otto's Irresistible Dance
spells.desc: Choose one creature within range. The target begins a comic dance in place: shuffling, tapping its feet, and capering for the duration. Creatures immune to charm are unaffected. A dancing creature must use all its movement to dance, has disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws and attack rolls, and grants advantage to attackers.

spells.name: Planar Ally
spells.desc: You beseech an otherworldly entity for aid. The entity can be a good deity, evil deity, a powerful elemental, or another cosmic power. The DM chooses what form the ally takes. The ally might be a celestial, an elemental, or a fiend. You must negotiate with it for its service.

spells.name: Programmed Illusion

material.desc: a bit of fleece and jade dust worth at least 25 GP

spells.desc: You create an illusion of an object, a creature, or some other visible phenomenon within range that activates when a specific condition occurs. A creature can detect the illusion by succeeding on an Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC.

spells.name: Sunbeam

material.desc: a magnifying glass

spells.desc: A beam of brilliant light flashes out from your hand in a 5-foot-wide, 60-foot-long line. Each creature in the line must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 6d8 radiant damage and is blinded until your next turn. Undead and oozes have disadvantage. On a success, half damage.

spells.name: Transport via Plants
spells.desc: This spell creates a magical link between a Large or larger inanimate plant within range and another plant, at any distance, on the same plane. Any creature can step into the target plant and exit from the destination plant by using 5 feet of movement.

spells.name: True Seeing

material.desc: an ointment made from mushroom powder, saffron, and fat worth 25 GP

spells.desc: You give the willing creature you touch the ability to see things as they actually are. For the duration, the creature has truesight, can see into the Ethereal Plane, and notices secret doors hidden by magic, all out to 120 feet.

spells.name: Wall of Ice

material.desc: a small piece of quartz

spells.desc: You create a wall of ice on a solid surface within range. The wall is 1 foot thick and up to 30 feet long and 10 feet high, or it's a hemispherical dome up to 20 feet in diameter. The wall lasts for the duration. Each 10-foot section has AC 12 and 30 hit points. Creatures in the wall's space take 10d6 cold damage.

spells.name: Wall of Thorns

material.desc: a handful of thorns

spells.desc: You create a wall of tough, pliable, tangled brush bristling with needle-sharp thorns. The wall appears within range on a solid surface and lasts for the duration. You choose to make the wall up to 60 feet long, 10 feet high, and 5 feet thick or a circle with a diameter of up to 20 feet. Creatures entering take 7d8 piercing damage.

spells.name: Wind Walk

material.desc: fire and holy water

spells.desc: You and up to ten willing creatures you can see within range assume a gaseous form for the duration, appearing as wisps of cloud. While in this cloud form, a creature has a flying speed of 300 feet and has resistance to damage from nonmagical weapons.

spells.name: Word of Recall
spells.desc: You and up to five willing creatures within 5 feet of you instantly teleport to a previously designated sanctuary. You establish the sanctuary by casting this spell in the location you want to designate, while attuned to a specific place of worship to your deity. After you cast the spell in the sanctuary, you and up to 5 willing creatures within 5 feet can teleport to it.

spells.name: Conjure Celestial
spells.desc: You summon a celestial of challenge rating 4 or lower, which appears in an unoccupied space that you can see within range. If you lose concentration, the celestial doesn't disappear; it acts according to its nature.

spells.name: Delayed Blast Fireball

material.desc: a tiny ball of bat guano and sulfur

spells.desc: A beam of yellow light flashes from your pointing finger, then condenses to linger at a chosen point within range as a glowing bead for the duration. When the spell ends—either because your concentration is broken or because you decide to end it—the bead blossoms with a low roar into an explosion of flame that spreads around corners. Each creature in a 20-foot-radius sphere must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking at minimum 12d6 fire damage.

spells.name: Divine Word
spells.desc: You utter a divine word, imbued with the power that shaped the world. Choose any number of creatures you can see within range. Each creature that can hear you must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, a creature suffers an effect based on its current hit points: 50 or fewer—deafened; 40 or fewer—blinded and deafened; 30 or fewer—stunned, blinded, deafened; 20 or fewer—killed instantly.

spells.name: Etherealness
spells.desc: You step into the border regions of the Ethereal Plane, where you remain for the duration. For the duration, you can move in any direction—up, down, or sideways. If you move up or down, every foot of movement costs an extra foot. You can see and hear the Material Plane, but everything looks gray, and you can't see anything more than 60 feet away. You can only affect and be affected by other creatures on the Ethereal Plane.

spells.name: Finger of Death
spells.desc: You send negative energy coursing through a creature that you can see within range, causing it searing pain. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. It takes 7d8+30 necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. A humanoid killed by this spell rises at the start of your next turn as a zombie that is permanently under your command.

spells.name: Fire Storm
spells.desc: A storm made up of sheets of roaring flame appears in a location you choose within range. The area of the storm consists of up to ten 10-foot cubes, which you can arrange as you wish. Each cube must have at least one face adjacent to the face of another cube. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 7d10 fire damage on a failed save, or half on a success.

spells.name: Forcecage

material.desc: ruby dust worth 1,500 GP

spells.desc: An immobile, invisible, cube-shaped prison composed of magical force springs into existence around an area you choose within range. The prison can be a cage (bars 1/2 inch thick, 1/2 inch apart) or a box (solid walls). A creature inside the cage can't leave by nonmagical means. The cage lasts for the duration.

spells.name: Mirage Arcane
spells.desc: You make terrain in an area up to 1 mile square look, sound, smell, and even feel like some other sort of terrain. Open fields or a road could be made to resemble a swamp, hill, crevasse, or some other difficult or impassable terrain. A pond can be made to seem like a grassy meadow, a precipice like a gentle slope, or a rock-strewn gully like a wide and smooth road.

spells.name: Mordenkainen's Magnificent Mansion

material.desc: a miniature portal carved from ivory, a small piece of polished marble, and a tiny silver spoon, worth 15 GP each

spells.desc: You conjure an extradimensional dwelling in range that lasts for the duration. You choose where its one entrance is located. The entrance shimmers faintly and is 5 feet wide and 10 feet tall. You and any creature you designate when you cast the spell can enter the extradimensional dwelling.

spells.name: Mordenkainen's Sword

material.desc: a miniature platinum sword with a grip and crossguard of copper and zinc, worth 250 GP

spells.desc: You create a sword-shaped plane of force that hovers within range. It lasts for the duration. When the sword appears, you make a melee spell attack against a target of your choice within 5 feet of the sword. On a hit, the target takes 3d10 force damage. Until the spell ends, you can use a bonus action on each of your turns to move the sword up to 20 feet and repeat the attack.

spells.name: Plane Shift

material.desc: a forked metal rod worth 250 GP attuned to a specific plane

spells.desc: You and up to eight willing creatures who link hands in a circle are transported to a different plane of existence. You can specify a target destination in general terms, such as the City of Brass on the Elemental Plane of Fire or the palace of Dispater on the second level of the Nine Hells. Alternatively, you can use the spell as an attack to banish an unwilling creature.

spells.name: Prismatic Spray
spells.desc: Eight rays of light flash from your hand. Each ray is a different color and has a different power. Each creature in a 60-foot cone must make a Dexterity saving throw. For each target, roll a d8 to determine which color ray affects it: 1 red (10d6 fire), 2 orange (10d6 acid), 3 yellow (10d6 lightning), 4 green (death save), 5 blue (petrification), 6 indigo (restrained), 7 violet (blind), 8 special.

spells.name: Project Image

material.desc: a small replica of you made from materials worth 5 GP

spells.desc: You create an illusory copy of yourself that lasts for the duration. The copy can appear at any location within range that you have seen before, regardless of intervening obstacles. You can use your action to move this illusory copy up to twice your speed, and make it gesture, speak, and behave in whatever way you choose.

spells.name: Regenerate

material.desc: a prayer wheel and holy water

spells.desc: You touch a creature and stimulate its natural healing ability. The target regains 4d8+15 hit points. For the duration of the spell, the target regains 1 hit point at the start of each of its turns. The target's severed body members (fingers, legs, tails, etc.) are restored after 2 minutes.

spells.name: Resurrection

material.desc: a diamond worth 1,000 GP, which the spell consumes

spells.desc: You touch a dead creature that has been dead for no more than a century, that didn't die of old age, and that isn't undead. If its soul is free and willing, the target returns to life with all its hit points. This spell also neutralizes any poisons and cures normal diseases affecting the creature when it died.

spells.name: Reverse Gravity

material.desc: a lodestone and iron filings

spells.desc: This spell reverses gravity in a 50-foot-radius, 100-foot-high cylinder centered on a point within range. All creatures and objects not somehow anchored to the ground in the area fall upward and reach the top of the area. A creature can make a Dexterity saving throw to grab onto a fixed object it can reach.

spells.name: Sequester

material.desc: a powder composed of diamond, emerald, ruby, and sapphire dust worth 5,000 GP

spells.desc: By means of this spell, a willing creature or an object can be hidden away, safe from detection for the duration. When you cast the spell and touch the target, it becomes invisible and can't be targeted by divination spells or perceived through scrying sensors. The spell puts the target into a state of suspended animation.

spells.name: Simulacrum

material.desc: snow or ice in quantities sufficient to make a life-size copy of the duplicated creature; some hair, fingernail clippings, or other piece of the creature's body placed inside the snow or ice; and powdered ruby worth 1,500 GP, sprinkled over the duplicate and consumed by the spell

spells.desc: You shape an illusory duplicate of one beast or humanoid within range. The duplicate is a creature, partially real and formed from ice or snow, and it can take actions and otherwise be affected as a normal creature. It appears to be the same as the original, but it has half the creature's hit point maximum and is formed without any equipment. The simulacrum lacks the ability to learn or become more powerful.

spells.name: Symbol

material.desc: mercury, phosphorus, and powdered diamond and opal worth 1,000 GP each, which the spell consumes

spells.desc: When you cast this spell, you inscribe a harmful glyph on a surface. The glyph can cover an area no larger than 10 feet in diameter. When triggered, the symbol unleashes a magical effect. Choose from: Death, Discord, Fear, Hopelessness, Insanity, Pain, Sleep, or Stunning.

spells.name: Teleport
spells.desc: This spell instantly transports you and up to eight willing creatures of your choice that you can see within range, or a single object that you can see within range, to a destination you select. The destination can be anywhere on the same plane. Roll on the Teleport table to determine how accurate the teleportation is, based on familiarity.

spells.name: Antimagic Field

material.desc: a pinch of powdered iron or iron filings

spells.desc: A 10-foot-radius invisible sphere of antimagic surrounds you. This area is divorced from the magical energy that suffuses the multiverse. Within the sphere, spells can't be cast, summoned creatures disappear, and even magic items become mundane. Constructs and undead are not affected by the field.

spells.name: Antipathy/Sympathy

material.desc: either a lump of alum soaked in vinegar for the antipathy effect or a drop of honey for the sympathy effect

spells.desc: This spell attracts or repels creatures. You target something within range, either a Huge or smaller object or creature or an area no larger than a 200-foot cube. Specify a kind of intelligent creature. Creatures of that kind must make a Wisdom saving throw or be repelled (antipathy) or compelled to approach (sympathy).

spells.name: Clone

material.desc: a diamond worth 1,000 GP and at least 1 cubic inch of flesh of the creature to be cloned, which the spell consumes, and a vessel worth 2,000 GP

spells.desc: This spell grows an inert duplicate of a living creature as a safeguard against death. The clone develops to full maturity after 120 days. If the original creature dies, its soul transfers to the clone, provided the soul is free and willing to return. The clone is physically identical to the original.

spells.name: Control Weather

material.desc: burning incense and bits of earth and wood mixed in water

spells.desc: You take control of the weather within 5 miles of you for the duration. You must be outdoors to cast this spell. Moving to a place where you don't have a clear path to the sky ends the spell early. When you cast the spell, you change the current weather conditions, which are determined by the DM based on the climate and season.

spells.name: Demiplane
spells.desc: You create a shadowy door on a flat solid surface that you can see within range. The door is large enough to allow Medium creatures to pass through unhindered. When opened, the door leads to a demiplane that appears to be an empty room 30 feet in any dimension. The demiplane lasts for the duration.

spells.name: Dominate Monster
spells.desc: You attempt to beguile a creature that you can see within range. It must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you for the duration. While charmed, you have a telepathic link with it. Each time it takes damage, it makes a new Wisdom saving throw.

spells.name: Earthquake

material.desc: a pinch of dirt, a piece of rock, and a lump of clay

spells.desc: You create a seismic disturbance at a point on the ground you can see within range. For the duration, an intense tremor rips through the ground in a 100-foot-radius circle centered on that point. The spell affects the ground and creatures on it, causing difficult terrain, concentration checks, and potential collapse of structures.

spells.name: Feeblemind

material.desc: a handful of clay, crystal, glass, or mineral spheres

spells.desc: You blast the mind of a creature you can see within range, attempting to shatter its intellect and personality. The target takes 4d6 psychic damage and must make an Intelligence saving throw. On a failed save, the creature's Intelligence and Charisma scores become 1. The creature can't cast spells, activate magic items, or communicate intelligibly. At the end of every 30 days, the target can repeat its saving throw.

spells.name: Glibness
spells.desc: Until the spell ends, when you make a Charisma check, you can replace the number you roll with a 15. Additionally, no matter what you say, magic that would determine if you are telling the truth indicates that you are being truthful.

spells.name: Holy Aura

material.desc: a tiny reliquary worth 1,000 GP containing a sacred relic

spells.desc: Divine light washes out from you and coalesces in a soft radiance in a 30-foot radius around you. Creatures of your choice in that radius when you cast this spell shed dim light in a 5-foot radius. Aura grants advantage on saving throws, and other creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls against them. Fiends and undead that hit a target in the aura must make a Constitution save or be blinded.

spells.name: Incendiary Cloud
spells.desc: A swirling cloud of smoke shot through with white-hot embers appears in a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on a point within range. The cloud spreads around corners and is heavily obscured. It lasts for the duration or until a wind of moderate or greater speed (at least 10 miles per hour) disperses it. When the cloud appears, each creature in it must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 10d8 fire damage.

spells.name: Maze
spells.desc: You banish a creature you can see within range into a labyrinthine demiplane. The target remains there for the duration or until it escapes. The target can use its action to attempt to escape by making a DC 20 Intelligence check. Minotaurs and labyrinths are related, and a minotaur automatically succeeds.

spells.name: Mind Blank
spells.desc: Until the spell ends, one willing creature you touch is immune to psychic damage, any effect that would sense its emotions or read its thoughts, divination spells, and the charmed condition.

spells.name: Power Word Stun
spells.desc: You speak a word of power that overwhelms the mind of one creature you can see within range, leaving it dumbfounded. If the target has 150 hit points or fewer, it is stunned. Otherwise, the spell has no effect. The stunned target must make a Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns; on a successful save, the stun ends.

spells.name: Sunburst

material.desc: fire and a piece of sunstone

spells.desc: Brilliant sunlight blazes in a 60-foot radius centered on a point you choose within range. Each creature in that light must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 12d6 radiant damage and is blinded for 1 minute. Undead and oozes have disadvantage on this saving throw. Blindness ends when the target succeeds on a Constitution saving throw.

spells.name: Telepathy

material.desc: a pair of linked silver rings

spells.desc: You create a telepathic link between yourself and a willing creature with which you are familiar. The creature can be anywhere on the same plane of existence as you. The spell ends if you or the target are no longer on the same plane. Until the spell ends, you and the target can instantaneously share words, images, sounds, and other sensory messages with one another.

spells.name: Tsunami
spells.desc: A wall of water springs into existence at a point you choose within range. You can make the wall up to 300 feet long, 300 feet high, and 50 feet thick. The wall lasts for the duration. When the wall appears, each creature within its area must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 6d10 bludgeoning damage, or half as much on a successful save.

spells.name: Astral Projection

material.desc: for each creature you affect with this spell, you must provide one jacinth worth at least 1,000 GP and one ornately carved bar of silver worth at least 100 GP, all of which the spell consumes

spells.desc: You and up to eight willing creatures within range project your astral bodies into the Astral Plane. You leave your bodies behind in a state of suspended animation. While your spirit is in the Astral Plane, your astral body is connected to your physical body by a silvery cord.

spells.name: Foresight

material.desc: a hummingbird feather

spells.desc: You touch a willing creature and bestow a limited ability to see into the immediate future. For the duration, the target can't be surprised and has advantage on attack rolls, ability checks, and saving throws. Additionally, other creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls against the target for the duration.

spells.name: Gate

material.desc: a diamond worth 5,000 GP

spells.desc: You conjure a portal linking an unoccupied space you can see within range to a precise location on a different plane of existence. The portal is a circular opening, up to 20 feet in diameter. You can orient the portal in any direction you choose. The portal lasts for the duration. Alternatively, you can speak the name of a specific creature to call it through.

spells.name: Imprisonment

material.desc: a vellum depiction or carved statuette in the likeness of the target, and a special component that varies according to the version of the spell worth 500 GP per HD of the target

spells.desc: You create a magical restraint to hold a creature within range. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it is bound by the spell and can't take any action. Various versions exist: Burial, Chaining, Hedged Prison, Minimus Containment, or Slumber. A wish spell can free the creature.

spells.name: Mass Heal
spells.desc: A flood of healing energy flows from you into injured creatures around you. You restore up to 700 hit points, divided as you choose among any number of creatures that you can see within range. Creatures healed this way are also cured of all diseases and any effect making them blinded or deafened.

spells.name: Meteor Swarm
spells.desc: Blazing orbs of fire plummet to the ground at four different points you can see within range. Each creature in a 40-foot-radius sphere centered on each point must make a Dexterity saving throw. The sphere spreads around corners. A creature takes 20d6 fire damage and 20d6 bludgeoning damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.

spells.name: Power Word Heal
spells.desc: A wave of healing energy washes over the creature you touch. The target regains all its hit points. If the creature is charmed, frightened, paralyzed, or stunned, the condition ends. If the creature is prone, it can use its reaction to stand up. This spell has no effect on undead or constructs.

spells.name: Power Word Kill
spells.desc: You utter a word of power that can compel one creature you can see within range to die instantly. If the creature you choose has 100 hit points or fewer, it dies. Otherwise, the spell has no effect.

spells.name: Prismatic Wall
spells.desc: A shimmering multicolored plane of light forms a vertical opaque wall—up to 90 feet long, 30 feet high, and 1 inch thick—centered on a point you can see within range. Alternatively, you can shape the wall into a sphere up to 30 feet in diameter centered on a point you choose within range. The wall is composed of seven layers, each a different color. Each layer has different effects and damage types.

spells.name: Shapechange

material.desc: a jade circlet worth at least 1,500 GP, which you must place on your head before you cast the spell

spells.desc: You assume the form of a different creature for the duration. The new form can be of any creature with a challenge rating equal to your level or lower. You transform into the chosen creature. You retain your alignment, personality, Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores. You assume the hit points of your new form. When you revert to your normal form, you return to the number of hit points you had before you transformed.

spells.name: Storm of Vengeance
spells.desc: A churning storm cloud forms, centered on a point you can see and spreading to a radius of 360 feet. Lightning flashes in the area, thunder booms, and strong winds roar. Each round on your turn, the storm has different devastating effects depending on the round number (thunder, lightning, acidic hail, freezing rain, lethal hailstones).

spells.name: Time Stop
spells.desc: You briefly stop the flow of time for everyone but yourself. No time passes for other creatures, while you take 1d4+1 turns in a row, during which you can use actions and move as normal. This effect ends if one of the actions you use during this period, or any effects that you create during this period, affects a creature other than you or an object being worn or carried by someone other than you.

spells.name: True Polymorph

material.desc: a drop of mercury, a dollop of gum arabic, and a wisp of smoke

spells.desc: Choose one creature or nonmagical object that you can see within range. You transform the creature into a different creature, or a creature into a nonmagical object, or a nonmagical object into a creature. If you concentrate on this spell for the full duration, the transformation becomes permanent.

spells.name: True Resurrection

material.desc: a sprinkle of holy water and diamonds worth 25,000 GP, which the spell consumes

spells.desc: You touch a creature that has been dead for no longer than 200 years and that died for any reason except old age. If the creature's soul is free and willing, the creature is restored to life with all its hit points. This spell closes all wounds, neutralizes any poison, cures all diseases, and lifts any curses affecting the creature when it died.

spells.name: Weird
spells.desc: Drawing on the deepest fears of a group of creatures, you create illusory creatures in their minds, visible only to them. Each creature in a 30-foot-radius sphere centered on a point of your choice within range must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature becomes frightened for the duration. At the end of each of the target's turns, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or take 4d10 psychic damage.

spells.name: Wish
spells.desc: Wish is the mightiest spell a mortal creature can cast. By simply speaking aloud, you can alter the very foundations of reality. The basic use of this spell is to duplicate any other spell of 8th level or lower. You can instead create one of a number of other effects, such as granting 20 creatures up to 10 hit points, creating a nonmagical item worth up to 25,000 GP, or other great miracles.
